title: Legendary playback singer Asha Bhosle passes away in Mumbai | DD News On Air
content:
Legendary singer and Padma Vibhushan awardee Asha Bhosle passed away at the age of 92 in Mumbai today. Maharashtra Cultural Minister Ashish Shelar confirmed the news while speaking to the media outside the hospital. Her last rites will be held tomorrow at 4 PM at Shivaji Park. She was admitted to a private hospital in Mumbai yesterday evening due to a chest infection and exhaustion, as informed by her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle on social media.

Asha Bhosle was widely regarded as one of the greatest vocalists in modern Indian music, with a career spanning several decades. Born in 1933 into the renowned Mangeshkar family, she began her singing career at the age of nine. She recorded her first film song in 1943, and by the 1950s, she had established a distinct identity for herself in Hindi cinema. Over the decades, she remained one of the most sought-after playback singers, working with leading music composers across the industry.

Among her most popular songs are ‘Abhi Na Jao Chhod Kar’, ‘In Ankhon Ki Masti’, ‘Dil Cheez Kya Hai’, ‘Piya Tu Ab to Aaja’, ‘Duniya Mein Logon Ko’, and ‘Zaara Se Jhoom Loon Main’, among others. Bhosle recorded over 12,000 songs in different Indian languages and has been the voice for many leading ladies, from Meena Kumari, Madhubala, and Zeenat Aman, to Kajol, Urmila Matondkar and also South actors like Padmini and Vyjayanthimala.

title: Neha Dhupia Shares Why She Swears by Milk Daily—Expert Reveals How Much Is Truly Healthy
content:
Stay updated with latest explainers, opinions and much more. Unsubscribe any time from your settings

Actor and host Neha Dhupia recently sat down for an engaging conversation about life, children and nutrition with actor Rubina Dilaik on the latest season of her YouTube podcast. “People should stop saying that milk is not good for you. Milk is great for you. I drink milk all the time. I feel it’s so important as far as our children are also concerned,” said Dhupia, 45.

Taking a leaf out of her diary, we asked an expert for an informed insight.

DISCLAIMER: This article is based on information from the public domain and/or the experts we spoke to. Always consult your health practitioner before starting any routine.

Dt Amreen Sheikh, chief dietitian, KIMS Hospitals, Thane, said milk has long been viewed as a healthy, nutrient-rich drink, and for good reason. “It offers high-quality protein, calcium, vitamin B12, and other important nutrients that support bone health, muscle function, and overall wellness. For many, especially those with balanced diets and no intolerance, milk can be a healthy daily addition,” said Sheikh.

However, “great for all” isn’t quite accurate, she contended. “Nutrition is personal. Some people digest milk easily, while others may feel bloated or uncomfortable or have lactose intolerance. For those individuals, drinking milk regularly could cause more harm than good,” said Sheikh.

Moderation is essential. “One to two servings a day, around 200 to 400 ml, is generally enough for most adults. Drinking too much does not necessarily offer extra benefits and can lead to unwanted calorie intake, especially if you already meet your protein and calcium needs through other foods,” said Sheikh.

It’s also crucial to consider your overall diet. Milk should enhance a balanced diet, not replace whole foods like fruits, vegetables, and grains.

Yes, some groups may gain more from regular milk consumption. “Children, teenagers, and older adults often require more calcium and protein for growth and bone health. Likewise, those with higher protein needs, such as active individuals, might find milk a convenient and effective option,” said Sheikh.

However, alternatives like curd, buttermilk, paneer, or fortified plant-based options can also provide similar benefits, especially for those who avoid milk.

The most important thing is to listen to your body. “If milk works for you, it can be a helpful addition to your routine. But if you experience digestive issues or intolerance, exploring alternatives is a good idea instead of forcing it into your diet.”

It is also essential to understand that no single food is a magic solution.

DISCLAIMER: This article is based on information from the public domain and/or the experts we spoke to. Always consult your health practitioner before starting any routine.

title: 'I Am a Victim of My Own Success' – Invincible VS Fans Call for Rage Quitting to Be Penalized as Beta Comes to an End
content:
As the Invincible VS beta test winds down, players are hoping publisher Skybound Games and developer Quarter Up do something to fix its “rage quitting” problem.

The fighting game based on the comic book-turned-TV show will soon close the servers that have allowed players to try out a slice of its superhero roster since April 9. With launch still weeks away, those who signed up for the limited test are left to reflect on the battles where they may have beaten their opponents a bit too badly.

Players of varying skill levels flocked to the Invincible VS beta and quickly found that there seemed to be little penalty for those who left matches prematurely. It led to moments where Viltrumites and Guardians alike would be pummeling their foes with a string of combos only to be met with an error that simply said, “opponent disconnected.”

"If someone as casual as myself is experiencing this frequently then that's a bit worrisome when you think about how the playerbase will be for this game," one player said. "Safe to say we're going to need an automated penalty system to deter rage quitting in this game for sure."

“My trick for less quits was to start playing with random characters I didn’t really practice with,” another suggested. “That way there’s a feeling out process in the beginning of the fight that makes them less likely to rage quit.”

Rage quitters, otherwise known as players who exit a match early because they may or may not be losing, are far from a new concept. Whether it be through delayed queue times or even temporary bans, many multiplayer games, especially the more competitive ones, typically have some sort of system in place to discourage this kind of behavior. Invincible VS, however, doesn’t seem to have any kind of deterrent.

Some players believe matchmaking may help the issue, with the more skilled fighters also reporting that they’ve continuously found themselves stacked in unfair matches leaning in their favor. While others have started suggesting Quarter Up implement penalties of some kind, a few believe something more extravagant, such as a feature similar to the Quitality from Mortal Kombat.

It makes sense that a free beta test would attract players who aren't as familiar with tag-team fighting games like Invincible VS, so many fans have started recommending newcomers at least don't skip the tutorial. Either way, some of the memes that have emerged from the weekend suggest players want something to be done.

“Make a fighting game free and you get a bunch of people who arent prepared for a fighting game,” one Reddit user teased.

"God forbid I have prior fighting game experience, lol," another joked.

Invincible VS launches for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X | S April 30, 2026. Those who participated in the beta gained access to the Coalition of Planets Omni-Man skin. For more while we wait to see how Quarter Up and Skybound may respond to complaints of rage quitting and other beta feedback, you can learn more about Conquest as a playable character and the two DLC fighters set to arrive this summer.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

title: Artificial Sweeteners May Harm Future Generations, Study Suggests
content:
A new study suggests that widely used zero-calorie sweeteners may subtly reshape the gut microbiome and alter gene activity linked to metabolism and inflammation.

Opting for a diet soda may seem like a simple way to cut calories, but the trade-off is replacing sugar with non-nutritive sweeteners such as sucralose or stevia.

These compounds deliver sweetness without energy, yet scientists are increasingly questioning whether “zero-calorie” also means biologically neutral. Studies suggest these additives may influence how the body regulates energy, raising concerns about possible links to metabolic disorders over time.

A new mouse study adds another layer to that debate, pointing to subtle but lasting changes in the gut and at the genetic level. Researchers found that exposure to sucralose or stevia altered the gut microbiome and affected genes tied to metabolism and inflammation. In some cases, these changes appeared to persist into the next generation, hinting that diet could have biological effects that extend beyond a single lifetime.

“We found it intriguing that despite the growing consumption of these additives, the prevalence of obesity and metabolic disorders such as insulin resistance has not declined,” said Dr. Francisca Concha Celume of the Universidad de Chile, lead author of the article in Frontiers in Nutrition. “This does not mean that sweeteners are responsible for these trends, but it raises the question of whether they influence metabolism in ways we do not yet fully understand.”

The researchers divided 47 male and female mice into three groups. One group received plain water, while the others were given water containing sucralose or stevia at levels similar to typical human intake. These mice were then bred for two generations, and their offspring were given only plain water.

“Animal models allow us to control environmental conditions very precisely and to isolate the effect of a specific factor, such as a dietary compound, while also following several generations within a relatively short time,” explained Concha.

Each generation underwent an oral glucose tolerance test, which measures insulin resistance, a warning sign for diabetes. The team also collected fecal samples to examine changes in the gut microbiome and levels of short-chain fatty acids. These compounds can reflect epigenetic changes that may be passed from parents to offspring. Sweeteners are thought to influence these fatty acids by disrupting the gut microbiome, which can in turn affect gene expression.

In addition, the researchers analyzed the activity of five genes linked to inflammation, gut barrier function, and metabolism in the liver and intestines. This provided a snapshot of how non-nutritive sweeteners might influence biological pathways related to metabolic and inflammatory health.

The results showed that the two sweeteners produced distinct and evolving effects. In the first generation, only male offspring of mice exposed to sucralose showed impaired glucose tolerance. By the second generation, higher fasting blood sugar levels appeared in male descendants of the sucralose group and female descendants of the stevia group.

Both sweetener groups showed increased diversity in their gut microbiomes but lower levels of short-chain fatty acids, suggesting reduced production of beneficial metabolites. These reductions persisted in later generations. The effects were more pronounced and longer lasting in mice exposed to sucralose, which showed a shift toward more harmful bacterial species and fewer beneficial ones.

Sucralose also appeared to increase the activity of genes associated with inflammation while reducing the activity of genes involved in metabolism, with effects lasting up to two generations. Stevia influenced gene expression as well, but its effects were milder and did not extend beyond one generation.

“When we compared generations, these effects were generally strongest in the first generation and tended to decrease in the second generation,” said Concha. “Overall, the effects linked to sucralose were more consistent and persistent across generations.”

“The changes we observed in glucose tolerance and gene expression could be interpreted as early biological signals related to metabolic or inflammatory processes,” said Concha. “For example, the animals did not develop diabetes. Instead, what we observed were subtle changes in how the body regulates glucose and in the activity of genes associated with inflammation and metabolic regulation. It is possible that such changes could increase susceptibility to metabolic disturbances under certain conditions, such as a high-fat diet.”

The researchers stress that these findings show associations rather than direct cause and effect. They also note that results from mice do not necessarily apply to humans.

“The goal of this research is not to create alarm, but to highlight the need for further investigation,” said Concha. “It may be reasonable to consider moderation in the consumption of these additives and to continue studying their long-term biological effects.”

Reference: “Artificial and natural non-nutritive sweeteners drive divergent gut and genetic responses across generations” by Francisca Concha Celume, Francisco Pérez-Bravo, Fabien Magne, Ricardo Olivares and Martin Gotteland, 30 January 2026, Frontiers in Nutrition.
DOI: 10.3389/fnut.2026.1694149

Funding: Fondo Nacional de Investigación y Desarrollo en Salud, Universidad de Chile

title: AI nephrology 2026: Dialysis advances raise ethics, clinical concerns
content:
Bengaluru: As Artificial Intelligence begins assisting treatments such as dialysis in government hospitals, questions emerge about how far these systems can align with clinical judgement and patient care.

Doctors broadly agree that AI is a useful tool in treatment and diagnosis. Yet, it also raises ethical dilemmas.

AI can play a major role in detecting chronic kidney disease (CKD) faster than traditional tests.

Speaking to DH, Dr Topoti Mukherjee, Lead Consultant, Nephrology and Kidney Transplant at a private hospital, said, "Some of the recent studies indicate that deep learning algorithms can identify CKD risk factors as much as 6 to 12 months prior compared with clinical diagnosis."

"In addition, Artificial Intelligence imaging using convolutional neural networks for renal scan analysis has been able to detect abnormalities in kidney structure that are not visible via traditional imaging methods," she said.

This shift from reactive diagnosis to predictive risk stratification is particularly valuable in CKD, which is often asymptomatic in its early stages.

Addressing the conflict between AI assessment and clinical assessment, Dr Arun Kumar J, Consultant Nephrologist, said, "The conflict does happen and there are times when AI may not quite fit the clinical situation. Sometimes AI is picking up a trend that is not obvious at first glance."

"At the end of the day, the responsibility sits with the clinician. So, the final call has to come from us. AI can inform the decision, but it does not make it," he added.

AI is transforming dialysis by enabling more precise, personalised and safer treatment. It continuously monitors patient data such as blood pressure, fluid levels and toxin removal, predicts complications, and supports doctors in adjusting care.

This improves outcomes and reduces emergencies. It also lowers manual workload and enhances efficiency in dialysis centres, said Dr Kishan A, Consultant, Nephrology.

On the limitations of AI, he said, "However, its effectiveness depends on data quality, and it may miss sudden or rare complications. High costs, data privacy concerns, and the need for staff training remain challenges. AI cannot replace clinical judgement and requires constant human oversight to ensure safe, reliable and equitable dialysis care."

Dr Anoop M Gowda MD, DNB (Nephrology), Senior Consultant Nephrologist and Transplant Physician, said, "Using AI in healthcare raises ethical and practical concerns. Patient care largely relies on human interaction, which technology cannot replace. Questions of accountability remain unclear if outcomes are poor — whether responsibility lies with the doctor, developer, or system."

"AI also depends on costly infrastructure and data often drawn from Western populations, limiting relevance in India. While promising, AI must be used cautiously alongside clinical judgement, with doctors ultimately guiding decisions and intervening, when necessary," he said.

title: Will Forza Horizon 6 Be On Xbox Game Pass?
content:
Forza Horizon 6 hits Game Pass day one on May 19, and Ultimate & PC subscribers play free. Here's how to pre-install it early.

Join 100K+ fans and never miss breaking news, fan theories, trailers & exclusive
                content.

Forza Horizon 6 officially comes out on May 19, 2026, and yes, it will be on Xbox Game Pass; however, it will be limited to a particular tier of subscription. The upcoming game, set in the much-awaited world of Japan and full of racing culture references from around the country, will be available on Xbox Series X/ S and PC on launch, with a release on PlayStation 5 coming later this year.

Here’s everything you need to know about Forza Horizon 6 coming to Game Pass, from confirming its availability to which specific plans include the game.

Forza Horizon 6 will be available on Xbox Game Pass on day one. Developed by Playground Games, it officially comes out on May 19, 2026, and both Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscribers will be able to download and play the Standard Edition at no additional cost from launch day itself.

The release of Forza Horizon 6 as a day-one title is no surprise, thanks to Microsoft’s long-standing tradition of releasing first-party titles directly into Game Pass on day one, making it one of the best deals in gaming for Forza fans who don’t want to pay the full retail price of $69.99 for the Standard Edition.

Additionally, for Game Pass Ultimate members, gamers will also have access to Forza Horizon 6 via Xbox Cloud Gaming, meaning you can stream the game on supported devices without needing to download it at all.

For those who want to play the game early, there is an option to purchase the Premium Upgrade, which unlocks a four-day early access starting May 15, 2026, along with additional content, including, but not limited to, VIP Membership, Car Pass, Welcome Pack, two post-launch expansions, and more.

Pre-installing Forza Horizon 6 through Xbox Game Pass is as simple as it gets, and it makes sure you are ready to jump into action the moment the game comes out on May 19. Here’s how to do it:

Forza Horizon 6 is also available on Steam. However, Game Pass access is not redeemable through Steam. It’s also important to make sure your Game Pass subscription remains active on May 19 to access the game without interruption.

title: Hungary's Viktor Orban, A Trump Ally, Loses Polls After 16 Years In Power
content:
Nationalist Viktor Orban, who has ruled Hungary for 16 years, has conceded defeat to conservative Peter Magyar in parliamentary elections. With votes in almost 67 percent of precincts counted, Magyar's party stood to have gained 137 seats, or more than two-thirds of all 199 parliamentary seats, according to official election results.

The vote Sunday was being closely watched across Europe and in the United States, with US President Donald Trump endorsing Orban, a self-described "thorn" in the EU's side and a defender of "illiberal democracy" who is also close to Russian President Vladimir Putin.

"The election results, though not yet final, are clear and understandable; for us, they are painful but unambiguous," Orban, 62, told reporters. "We have not been entrusted with the responsibility and opportunity to govern. I congratulated the winning party," he said.

Magyar, 45, a political newcomer and former government insider, posed on social media that Orban "just called to congratulate us on our victory".

Turnout in the election reached a record 77.8 percent at 6:30 pm (1630 GMT).

"I'm really excited. I came to this event full of hope... I'm optimistic about the change," Orsolya Rozgonyi, a 28-year-old HR manager, told AFP amid Tisza supporters who gathered in Budapest in a festive atmosphere.

The atmosphere was more subdued among hundreds of Fidesz supporters who gathered near the press centre, cordoned off from the press.

Orban, who was seeking a fifth straight term, has transformed his country into a model of "illiberal democracy", publicly clashing with Brussels over rule-of-law issues, as well as over support for war-torn Ukraine.

Magyar burst onto the scene just two years ago, promising to fight corruption and offering better public services, amassing support against a backdrop of economic stagnation, despite an electoral system skewed in favour of Orban's Fidesz party.

Both camps have alleged foreign interference during the campaign in the central European country of 9.5 million people.

US Vice President JD Vance visited Hungary this week to rally with Orban, attacking the alleged interference in Hungary of Brussels "bureaucrats", while Trump has promised to bring US "economic might" to Hungary if Orban's party secures victory.

After casting his ballot, Orban repeated warnings of a "major crisis" awaiting Europe. "Fortunately we have a lot of friends in the world. From America to China to Russia and the Turkish world," he said.

Orban has focused on making Ukraine the central topic of his campaign, portraying the neighbouring country, which is fighting off a Russian invasion, as "hostile" to Hungary. He also vowed to continue his crackdown against "fake civil society organisations, bought journalists, judges (and) politicians".

Maria Toth, a 31-year-old stay-at-home mother of two, told AFP at a Budapest polling station that "it is so important for us that Viktor Orban stays in power". "I feel Hungary is under siege from so many directions and big powers like Brussels are trying to dictate how we live," she added.

Orban's chief of staff Gergley Gulyas said earlier Sunday that he didn't see, for now, "any infringement that would put the final result in question".

After voting, Magyar called on voters to report suspected vote-buying, intimidation or other violations, while urging calm.

Hansi Flick faces a major selection dilemma ahead of Atletico Madrid clash | Barca Universal
content:
Barcelona head into Tuesday’s second leg against Atletico Madrid with renewed optimism, and much of that stems from an unexpected source.

Hansi Flick had largely settled on his attacking setup heading into the Metropolitano, but Ferran Torres may have just complicated those plans in the best way possible.

The Valencian forward delivered a superb display against Espanyol in the derby, netting twice in the first half to end a 14-game scoring drought that had stretched back to January.

The goals lifted his tally for the season to 18, making him the third-highest scorer in the squad behind Lamine Yamal and Raphinha.

According to Diario SPORT, Flick may now have a genuine selection headache ahead of the second leg, with Torres pushing hard for a starting berth at the Metropolitano.

Robert Lewandowski had been the expected starter, having been rested entirely against Espanyol with Tuesday’s clash in mind. The Polish striker scored at the Metropolitano in La Liga just last week and remains a strong option for Flick.

However, Torres’ performance will be difficult to ignore. The forward also has a strong personal record against Atletico Madrid, having scored twice in a famous 2-4 comeback victory at the Metropolitano last season, as well as netting the decisive goal in the Copa del Rey semi-final second leg that sent Barcelona to the final.

After the full-time whistle on Saturday, Torres did not hold back when addressing his critics. “There is always external noise with me, always in the bad more than in the good,” he said, clearly motivated by the doubts that had surrounded him in recent weeks.

title: IPL 2026 - Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli troubled by injuries during RCB's win over MI | ESPNcricinfo
content:
MI batter went off on 19 and didn't return to bat, while Kohli was seen in the dugout at the end

Rohit Sharma retired hurt in the sixth over of the chase  •  Associated Press

Rohit Sharma retired hurt for Mumbai Indians (MI) while Virat Kohli went off the field with an ankle issue for Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) during the teams' IPL 2026 match in Mumbai on Sunday.

With MI chasing 241 at Wankhede, Rohit seemed to be limping four balls into the fifth over. Soon he needed treatment. But as much as he tried to bat on, it became clear that there was a problem. Rohit left the field with his hamstring all strapped up. He had made 19 off 13 balls and didn't return to bat as MI lost a high-scoring game by 18 runs.

By then, Kohli was already off the field for what Simon Doull described as an ankle injury on air. Kohli was seen getting treatment on his left leg five balls into the tenth over of RCB's innings. On 35 off 25 deliveries at that stage, he finished with 50 from 38 balls before being dismissed by Hardik Pandya in the 15th over.

Kohli was seen in the dugout as RCB completed the win over MI. Doull asked RCB captain Rajat Patidar about an update on Kohli's injury at the post-match presentation. "I don't know, but I feel he's okay right now," Patidar said.

The win has taken RCB to third position on the points table. They have now won three out of four games. MI, meanwhile, find themselves eighth after four games. They have one win so far and have followed that up with three successive defeats.

title: Eli Lilly's Retatrutide Shows Strong Weight Loss, Nears Diabetes Approval | Whalesbook
content:
Eli Lilly's retatrutide drug is making waves in the metabolic health field. It uniquely works by activating three key hormone receptors: GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon. This triple-agonist approach helps regulate appetite, energy balance, and metabolism. Recent Phase 3 trial data from TRIUMPH-4 showed participants lost up to 28.7% of their body weight over 68 weeks. For type 2 diabetes patients, retatrutide significantly lowered A1C levels, similar to tirzepatide, with average weight loss of 16.8% in 40 weeks. These findings position retatrutide as a potential next-generation treatment, especially for those not fully helped by existing drugs. Eli Lilly's stock (LLY), recently trading between $930 and $939, has seen a roughly 13% dip year-to-date, highlighting investor focus on its valuation as pipeline progress continues.

Retatrutide's promise extends beyond weight loss and blood sugar control, with research exploring its potential impact on longevity and broader metabolic health. This places it in the fast-growing longevity market. Its weight loss results have exceeded those of semaglutide (average 14.9% over 68 weeks) and tirzepatide (up to 22.5% over 72 weeks), giving it a clear advantage. The GLP-1 drug market is substantial, expected to grow from $13.84 billion in 2024 to $48.84 billion by 2030. Analysts remain largely positive, with price targets for Eli Lilly (LLY) around $1,200-$1,300, showing confidence in its pipeline, including retatrutide and the recently approved oral GLP-1 drug Foundayo.

Despite its clinical promise, retatrutide faces significant hurdles. The drug is still investigational, with potential approval unlikely before late 2027 or 2028. Seeking approval for its wide range of potential uses—including obesity, type 2 diabetes, knee osteoarthritis, and liver disease—adds complexity and extends the development timeline. The market is also becoming increasingly crowded. Strong competition from Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly's own tirzepatide means pricing and market access will be key. Concerns also exist that the extreme weight loss achieved by retatrutide could cause patient distress or require specialized metabolic support. Eli Lilly's valuation, with a P/E ratio around 40.6x, is high compared to industry averages, suggesting that anticipated future growth, including retatrutide's launch, is already factored into its stock price, leaving little room for setbacks.

Retatrutide represents a major growth opportunity for Eli Lilly, building on its strong position in diabetes and obesity treatments. title: From thalassemia to sickle cell: Bone marrow transplants as a ‘reset’ for genetic blood disorders – Firstpost
content:
For decades, the term Bone Marrow Transplant (BMT) has been synonymous with the battle against leukaemia and lymphoma. However, as medical science enters a new era, this life-saving procedure is shedding its cancer-only label.

In India, where the burden of hemoglobinopathies is immense with over 10,000 children born annually with Thalassemia major, BMT is emerging as a critical “reset button” for those with defective genetic blueprints.

According to Dr. (Maj) Ravi Shankar, Consultant – Paediatric Haemato-Oncology and BMT at Yatharth Hospital (Model Town) advances in medical science have expanded the role of BMT far beyond conditions like leukaemia and lymphoma. Today, it is being used to treat genetic and immune-related blood disorders such as thalassemia and sickle cell disease, which affect a large number of patients in India.

“In such cases, the problem lies not with cancer but with the bone marrow itself,” he explained. “A transplant effectively replaces a faulty system with a healthy one, acting as a reset for both blood production and the immune system.”

Experts underline that early diagnosis remains critical. Detecting genetic disorders through screening at an early stage can prevent irreversible organ damage and significantly improve outcomes. However, awareness alone is not enough, as access to timely treatment remains a challenge.

In India, BMT facilities are largely concentrated in major urban centres, limiting access for patients in smaller towns. The cost of the procedure—often running into tens of lakhs of rupees—poses a significant barrier for most families. Additionally, finding a compatible donor remains a major hurdle due to underdeveloped donor registries.

Despite these challenges, advancements in treatment are offering new hope. Doctors are increasingly using less intensive pre-transplant conditioning methods, making the procedure safer and more accessible to a wider group of patients. Emerging technologies such as gene-edited stem cells are also beginning to expand treatment possibilities, particularly for non-cancer patients.

The focus now, experts say, must be on bridging the gap between medical innovation and patient access, ensuring that these life-saving treatments reach those who need them most.

title: Persistent joint pain in children: It could be hemophilia, not a sports injury – Firstpost
content:
In the bustling playground of childhood, minor bumps and scrapes are a daily occurrence. However, when a child suddenly stops running or avoids putting weight on a specific limb, the cause may be far more complex than a playground tumble as it may indicate an underlying bleeding disorder such as hemophilia, a condition that is frequently overlooked in its early stages, leading to silent and progressive joint damage.

Recent epidemiological data for 2026 suggests that India remains a significant hub for hereditary bleeding disorders, yet thousands of pediatric cases go undiagnosed until irreversible joint damage has occurred. Identifying the “silent” internal bleeds of Hemophilia is now a top priority for hematologists aiming to curb long-term physical disability.

According to Dr Rahul Bhargava, Principal Director & Chief, Hematology, Hemato-Oncology & Bone Marrow Transplant, Fortis Memorial Research Institute, Gurgaon, hemophilia affects the body’s ability to form stable blood clots, resulting in internal bleeding episodes that most commonly occur in the knees, ankles and elbows.

“These bleeds happen inside the joint space, so they are not always visible like external injuries. Swelling, warmth and reduced movement are often the only early signs,” he explained.

A key concern is the recurring nature of these episodes. Joint bleeds tend to affect the same joint repeatedly, and each episode increases inflammation and gradually damages cartilage. Over time, this leads to stiffness, chronic pain and restricted mobility, affecting a child’s ability to walk, play and carry out normal activities.

Dr Bhargava noted that early episodes are often mistaken for simple injury-related swelling or growing pains. As a result, diagnosis is frequently delayed until joint damage has already begun, shifting the disease from a manageable condition to one that can cause lifelong disability.

Repeated bleeding not only weakens joint structure but can also lead to deformity and reduced range of motion. In severe cases, spontaneous bleeding may occur even without trauma, further increasing health risks.

However, early diagnosis can significantly change outcomes. A simple blood test to assess clotting factor levels can confirm hemophilia. Once diagnosed, clotting factor replacement therapy helps control bleeding episodes, while physiotherapy plays a crucial role in preserving joint function and improving mobility.

Experts stress that recognising patterns is critical—especially swelling that recurs in the same joint without clear injury. Increased awareness can help prevent irreversible damage and ensure children receive timely treatment during crucial years of growth and development.

For generations, marriages have been preceded by intense scrutiny of finances, professions, and astrological compatibility. However, as the 2026 wedding season approaches, a new trend is emerging: Pre-conception Carrier Screening. With India being home to a high burden of inherited blood disorders including an estimated 70–80 million carriers of Thalassemia, leading medical experts are urging couples to look past traditional checklists and focus on their shared genetic blueprint.


The 1-in-4 Risk: Understanding Silent Carriage


Most individuals harbor variations in their DNA without ever knowing it. According to Dr. Rashmi Talwar, Lab Head and Head of Genomics at Agilus Diagnostics, the risk arises when both partners unknowingly carry a mutation in the same gene.

"Carriers can be sound and asymptomatic, but they can transmit mutations to newborns," Dr. Talwar explains. "In such instances, every pregnancy carries a 1-in-4 risk of the baby developing a debilitating disability like Thalassemia or Cystic Fibrosis." This screening is a simple blood test that empowers couples to make informed reproductive choices, such as opting for IVF with genetic testing or specialized prenatal care.


The Hemophilia Factor: Breaking the X-Linked Silence


The urgency for screening is echoed by Dr. Rahul Bhargava, Chief of Hematology at Fortis Memorial Research Institute. He highlights Hemophilia as a critical example of an "unexpected" condition. Because it follows an X-linked pattern, women can carry the gene without visible symptoms, while their male children are likely to be affected.

"The absence of symptoms in carriers often delays testing until a child experiences excessive bleeding after a routine vaccination or minor injury," says Dr. Bhargava. He notes that screening before marriage or family planning shifts the approach from "uncertainty to preparedness," allowing deliveries to be planned in specialized centers equipped to handle newborns with bleeding disorders.


Normalising the Conversation


Both experts agree that the goal is not to "medicalise" marriage but to foster inclusion and preparedness. Family history remains a vital clinical tool; clusters of anemia, blood transfusions, or even certain leukemias within a family can provide an early roadmap for preventive care.

As diagnostics companies like Agilus normalise "Complete Care Pre-Marriage packages," the conversation is shifting. By identifying carrier status early, families can navigate potential challenges with clarity, ensuring that the next generation begins life with the best possible health foundation.

Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is increasingly being understood through a broader neurological lens, with emerging research pointing to possible links with other brain conditions, including Alzheimer’s disease. Experts, however, stress that early identification and intervention remain the most critical factors in improving outcomes for children.

“Early signs of autism often appear within the first two years of life,” said Dr. Supriya Malik, Developmental Psychologist and Head of Child Development Centre at Madhukar Rainbow Children’s Hospital. These may include limited eye contact, lack of response to one’s name, delayed speech, and reduced social engagement. Repetitive behaviours and sensory sensitivities are also commonly observed.

Dr. Malik emphasised that early recognition is not about labelling but enabling timely support. “When intervention begins early—ideally before the age of three—it can significantly improve language, behaviour, and independence,” she said, highlighting the role of speech therapy, occupational therapy, and behavioural interventions in shaping brain development.

Experts also caution against misinformation around autism risk factors. Concerns linking maternal infections to autism remain unproven. “There is no conclusive evidence establishing a direct causal link,” Dr. Malik noted, urging balanced, evidence-based communication to avoid unnecessary anxiety among expectant mothers.

At the same time, new research is expanding scientific understanding of autism. Dr. Avinash Kumar, Consultant Neurologist at Gleneagles Fortis Hospital, said studies have identified shared biological pathways between autism and Alzheimer’s, including disruptions in synaptic function and brain connectivity.

“This shifts the focus towards the health of neural networks rather than isolated disease markers,” he explained, adding that such findings could open new avenues for targeted therapies in the future. However, he stressed that the evidence remains preliminary and autism should not be viewed as a precursor to Alzheimer’s.

Experts underline that autism is a neurodevelopmental condition, not a disease to be cured. With timely support, inclusive care, and growing scientific insight, individuals on the spectrum can lead independent and fulfilling lives.

title: Outreach Ayushman Arogya Shivir Organised at GPS Austin-II – Andaman Chronicle
content:
Mayabunder, April 12: An outreach Ayushman Arogya Shivir was organized at Govt. Primary School Austin–II under AAM Mohanpur today to provide essential healthcare services to the local population. The camp was led by Dr. Alice Bwa, Medical Officer, Dr. R.P. Hospital, Mayabunder, along with a multidisciplinary team. As part of the 100 Days Campaign under TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan 2.0, various health services including screening for TB, NCDs, anaemia, malaria, and geriatric conditions were conducted. A yoga session and a psychological counselling session were also organized.

Dr. Alice Bwa, delivered an awareness talk on TB, HPV vaccination, leptospirosis, dengue fever, and heat-related illnesses among the general public. 45 beneficiaries attended the camp. Out of the 22 individuals screened through handheld X-ray, 3 cases were found abnormal. Additionally, 8 presumptive TB cases were identified and sent for sputum examination.

General medical consultations were provided, medicines were distributed, and patients requiring further evaluation were referred to Dr. R.P. Hospital, Mayabunder for further investigation including blood tests and sputum examination.

title: MI vs RCB Highlights, IPL 2026: Salt, Patidar shine; Royal Challengers Bengaluru beats Mumbai Indians by 18 runs - Sportstar
content:
MUMBAI, 12/03/2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s captain Rajat Patidar plays a shot during IPL Match Mumbai Indians vs Royal Challengers Bangalore at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai on Sunday. Photo: EMMANUAL YOGINI/ The Hindu | Photo Credit: EMMANUAL YOGINI

MI vs RCB, IPL 2026: Catch all the highlights from the IPL 2026 match between Mumbai Indians and Royal Challengers Bengaluru which happened at the Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai, on Sunday, April 12.

Romario Shepherd with the final over. High full toss to start with. Swatted away for a six by Rutherford. He gets to his fifty. He also wants a review for a no ball. Review shows the ball was below his waist height. Length ball now from Shepherd. Whacked away for a six to the leg side by Rutherford. Another six now. Over covers. He gets a fourth six. This time down the ground.

RCB has the last laugh though as it beats MI by 19 runs to get back to winning ways.

Low full toss from Bhuvi. Clipped away for a four by Rutherford. Bhuvi gets his length right for the rest of the over. RCB has practically sealed this game. Just nine runs from the over.

Duffy starts with a legside wide. And then gives away an off-side wide. RCB uses a review to overturn the call. Duffy with a low full toss. Swung away for a six by Rutherford. Another full toss. Another six. MI and RCB trade reviews to overturn wide calls. Rutherford closes the over with another six, over long on.

Bhuvi with the 17th over. Three runs from the first two balls. Short now from the RCB pacer. Santner guides it over backward point for a four. Two singles follow. A dot to finish the over. RCB has the win in sight.

Rasikh seems to have cramped up during his bowling run-up. And we have the strategic time out. Rutherford pumps the first ball after the break over mid wicket for a six. Rasikh is struggling. He needs medical attention again. But he has managed to continued. Back of a length and Naman Dhir slices it into the hands of extra cover.

With just one ball left, Rasikh is down on the floor again. He can’t finish the over. Shepherd steps up and he gets Rutherford out. Good running catch by Shepherd himself. Oh wait. He has overstepped. Rutherford survives. He gets a freehit to boot. Just a single off it though.

Duffy returns and he starts with a wide. Hardik slices one over extra cover for a double. Deft from Hardik now. Slices Duffy behind point for a four. Wide from Duffy. Hardik chases after it. Top edged and he is caught in the deep. This should be it for MI.

Suyash with his final over. Fires in a fuller one. Hardik whacks it down the ground. Krunal manages to get a hand onto it. But, such was the power that it spills over for a four. Rutherford also gets into the act. Slogs Suyash over deep mid wicket for a six. The required rate is well over 16 and MI needs more and more boundaries to keep the game within its reach.

Krunal vs Hardik again. Starts with an off-side wide. A single now brings SKY on strike. MI needs a biiiig over here. Instead they lose a big wicket. SKY is caught at deep fine leg attempting a lofted sweep.

Suryakumar Yadav c Rasikh Salam b Pandya 33 (22b 5x4 0x6)

MI brings in Rutherford as its impact sub. He gets off the mark with a single to the off side.

Suyash with a wide short ball to start the over. Hardik chases after it. Edged and runs away for a four. Hardik calls for a change of gloves. Hardik with a wild swing now. Beaten. Another change of gloves. Hauls the next to mid wicket for a single. SKY pumps the next to a similar region. Two runs now. Length now from Suyash. SKY cracks it through covers for a four.

Bhuvi returns and Hardik welcomes him with a four. Ticks away one off the pads for a single. Full now from Bhuvi. Pandya latches onto it. Smoked down the ground for a four. Hardik now misses out on a full toss. Drives a full one to long off for a single. Just nine runs from the over.

Suyash back into the attack. Suryakumar sweeps it over short fine leg, Salt from deep square leg tries to cut it off but the ball trickles to the boundary! FOUR RUNS!

Suryakumar cuts it on the off side for a BOUNDARY! Suryakumar hits it to backward point for a dot ball. Short and wide from Suyash, Suryakumar pulls it to long on for a single. Asking rate climbing up to 15 now.

Pandya cuts it fine through point for a BOUNDARY! That’s more like it from MI! Full from Suyash, Pandya hits it to long on for a single.

Krunal Pandya back into the attack. Suryakumar takes a, wait a minute that’s two runs on the off side. Suryakumar hits that to long off for a single.

Hardik on strike against his brother. Krunal flights that, Hardik hits it to long off for a single. Suryakumar goes for the sweep but that beats him and hits his pads, RCB appeals but its not given. RCB reviews. No bat involved.

That’s missing wickets, decision stays. Suryakumar hits it to deep cover for a single.

Hardik on strike, required rate more than 14 now. Krunal with a bouncer, Hardik leaves that alone.

Suyash Sharma into the attack. MI only three runs behind where RCB were at this stage. Rickelton sweeps but top edges it and the catch is taken by Bhuvneshwar at short third! SUYASH STRIKES ON HIS FIRST BALL! RICKELTON IS GONE!

Ryan Rickelton c Bhuvneshwar Kumar b Suyash Sharma 37(22) [4s-3 6s-3]

Tilak Varma is in at four. Defends. Tilak hits it to deep mid wicket for a single. Suryakumar flicks it to the cow corner region for a single.

Tilak sweeps that but its straight into the hands of Jacob Duffy at short third! SUYASH GETS TWO IN THE OVER! TILAK IS GONE!

Captain Hardik Pandya is in at five. GOES BIG FIRST BALL! PANDYA GOES OVER LONG OFF FOR A SIX!

Krunal back into the attack. Suryakumar flicks that nicely to the cow corner region for a double. Suryakumar guides it off his pads to long on for a single.

MI going at just over 10 rpo, will need to accelerate through the middle overs to stay in the chase.

Rickelton takes a single on the leg side. Suryakumar chips that in the air to long off for a single.

Rickelton gets down on one foot and fails to connect with the pull, takes a quick single. Suryakumar sweeps it over short fine leg for a BOUNDARY!

Rasikh to bowl the last over of the PowerPlay. Looks like Rohit Sharma has an issue here. The physio is out to check if he is fine. Good news, Rohit is fine. Play resumes. Rickelton on strike. Slower ball to start, Rickelton hits it to mid off for a quick single.

Rohit defends, dot ball. Rohit looks like he is struggling here and oh no, he’s walking off, looks its a hamstring issue. Rohit is retired hurt, big blow for MI.

Suryakumar Yadav is in at three. Suryakumar hits it to mid off, dot ball. Juicy full toss from Rasikh, Suryakumar easily hits that down the ground for a BOUNDARY!

Another slower ball from Rasikh, Suryakumar hits it to mid off, dot ball. Last ball of the Powerplay, full toss, and Suryakumar hits it to long on for a single.

Krunal Pandya into the attack. Rohit defends, dot ball. Full and wide from Krunal, Rohit reaches out to it and gets it to long off for a single.

Bouncer from Krunal, Rickelton pulls it to deep square leg, 50 up for MI. Rohit creates room but the ball goes off his pads to short fine leg, one leg bye. Rickelton guides it off his pads to deep mid wicket for a single.

Krunal goes full and Rohit carves it on the off side for a BOUNDARY!

Rasikh Dar into the attack. Rohit manages to get it just over mid on for a BOUNDARY! Change of pace from Rasikh, Rohit fails to connect with the cut, dot ball.

Low full toss from Rasikh, Rohit flicks it to deep square leg for a single. Oooohhh good slower ball from Rasikh, beats Rickelton’s outside edge.

Yorker from Rasikh, Rickelton hits it straight back to the bowler, dot ball. Full toss from Rasikh and Rickelton drives it through extra cover for a BOUNDARY!

Duffy with his second. Slower bouncer from Duffy, Rickelton pulls it in gap in the deep square region for a BOUNDARY! Short and wide from Duffy, Rickelton cuts it straight to cover, dot ball.

Rickelton pulls it over deep mid wicket for another SIX! Change of pace from Duffy, Rickelton hits it to mid on for a single. What a shot from Rohit! Charges and smashes it on the leg side all the way for SIX!

Change of pace from Duffy, Rohit cuts it to deep point for a single.

Bhuvneshwar Kumar to bowl the second over, Rohit on strike. Bhuvneshwar starts with a wide down leg side. Ooooh that’s a good yorker from Bhuvneshwar, Rohit gets back on that to mid wicket. Rohit pulls it to deep square leg for a single.

Half volley from Bhuvneshwar, Rickelton hits it to mid off for a single. Change of pace from Bhuvneshwar, Rohit hits it to mid on off his back foot for another single.

Rickelton effortlessly flicks it off his pads for a SIX! Rickelton drives it straight to mid off, he has to hurry for the single but eventually makes it.

Rohit and Rickelton walk out to open the batting for MI. Rasikh Dar is the impact sub for RCB, Tim David is subbed out. Jacob Duffy takes the new ball for RCB. Rickelton on strike, slip in place.

First ball, Rickelton hits it to mid off, dot ball. Oooohhh good length delivery from Duffy, beats Rickelton’s outside edge. Rickelton smashes it over deep square leg for a SIX!

Short and wide from Duffy, Rickelton can only cut it to cover. Rickelton tries to pull that but is beaten by Duffy’s short ball. Shot from Rickelton, punches it off his back foot through covers for a BOUNDARY!

RCB’s batters fired on all cylinders to post a commanding 240 on the board. MI has one of the most explosive batting lineups in the competition but a target of 241 is a tall ask for any team, even on the flattest of pitches. Openers Rohit Sharma and Ryan Rickelton will have no choice but to go big from ball one as the asking rate is more than 12 runs per over.

Can MI’s batting lineup pull off a sensational chase like they did against KKR when they chased down 221, or will RCB’s bowlers execute their plans to perfection? Expect fireworks, second innings to begin shortly..

Trent Boult with an inch-perfect yorker. Jitesh is trapped in front. No review for RCB.

Two more sharp yorkers from Boult. Two singles for RCB. Full again from Boult. Shepherd can only squeeze in a single. Finally, Boult misses his length. Full toss and smoked away for a six.

Bumrah with the penultimate over. Starts with a yorker. Leg-bye. Single. Low full toss to Jitesh. He can only get it to mid off. Another single. Full from Bumrah. Pummelled down the the ground for a four. Similar length again. On the pads this time though. Flicked away for a six. Just a single from the last two deliveries. Another wicketless game for Bumrah.

Wide bouncer from Hardik Pandya and Tim David flat-bats it over extra cover for a four. Pandya seems to have hurt himself while attempting that short ball. The physio is out in the middle and Pandya takes a breather.

He is back. But, he goes above waist-high against David. Pandya wants a review on the no ball. Clearly a no ball, on replay. Freehit for David. Full toss on the pads. Carted away for a six by David. Too easy for him. The umpire feels the necessity for a ball change. This could work in MI’s favour. Short from Hardik. Tim David can’t get it away. Just a single.

Naman Dhir with a good diving stop at the fence to prevent Jitesh a boundary. Two doubles to end the over.

Jitesh Sharma gets going with an inside out hit against Bumrah. Four! Bumrah responds with a slow bouncer. Jitesh is beaten. Now with the sucker punch. A full-blooded yorker. Jitesh manages to jam it away. Low full toss now. Jitesh can only clip it away for a single.

Santner with his final over of the day. Tim David is the new batter for RCB. Wide from Santner. Patidar slices at it. Edged and runs away for a four. That brings up his fifty and it has come up in just 17 deliveries. Terrific knock from Patidar. This is his second consecutive fifty.

But, Patidar can’t push any further. He holes out to long on attempting another six.

Rajat Patidar c Tilak Varma b Santner 53 (20b 4x4 5x6)

Kohli wastes another RCB review hoping to overturn a wide call. A pull gives Kohli a single and that brings up his fifty. Hardik sticks to a hard length. Patidar can only take a single. Low full toss from Hardik. No timing there for Kohli. Caught at long on.

Patidar is tearing this game apart. Full from Boult and it is slammed over mid off for a four. Similar length again. This time, the timing is even better. Over long off for a six. Boult gets his yorkers correct now. Patidar and Kohli can only squeeze them away for singles. Superb comeback from the Kiwi pacer after missing his range early in the over.

Full toss from Shardul and Patidar doesn’t missed out on this one. Swiped away for a four. Shardul struggling to control his line. Two wides early in the over. Slow bouncer now. That too is given a wide.

Full from Shardul. Patidar splices it over backward point for a sixes. RCB’s captain is out here with only one plan in his mind. Attack! RCB would love if Kohli follows suit. He is batting on 39 from 30. He gets in on the act with a double and then smacks a straight four. RCB has Tim David padded up and ready for furthering this onslaught.

RCB skipper is the new batter and he starts in a hurry. After dispatching Shardul for a first-ball four, he now goes after Mayank Markande. Back-to-back sixes against the leg spinner towards the legside. Patidar now with a reverse sweep. Perfectly timed. Over deep third for a six.

Markande with two low full tosses to close off the over. Patidar fails to put them away. Just a single from those two deliveries.

Shardul Thakur into the the attack. He starts with an off-side wide. He sticks to the same line. Kohli can’t reach enough to get any timing on it. Dot ball. Same line again. Wide again.

Shardul is clear with his plan. Bowl wide and away from the RCB batters. Another dot before Kohli slams one down the ground for a couple. Kohli steps across the stumps for a single to deep mid wicket.

Shardul pushes it wide again. Salt times it straight to Hardik Pandya at extra cover.

Hardik Pandya back into the attack. Salt tries to guide that past deep backward point but Markande runs from point and stops that, good fielding, he saves two runs for his team.

Salt goes for the pull but misses out and is beaten, dot ball. Full toss from Pandya looking for the yorker and Salt flicks it off his pads for a SIX ON THE LEG SIDE!

Oooohhhh Salt outside egdes that and it goes up in the air and it falls justttt short of Trent Boult at deep cover, single. Kohli flicks it to cow corner, Salt wants the second but is sent back by Kohli, its close at the strikers end, Rickelton takes the bail but Salt’s dive saves him.

What a start its been for RCB so far, no wickets lost and it has raced to 115 runs in ten overs. Dot to finish.

Santner with his third, Kohli strike. Starts with a full one, Kohli hits that to extra cover, dot ball. Kohli sweeps that and its in the airrr, doesn’t carry to Suryakumar Yadav at long on, single. Salt creates room and cuts that to deep cover for a single, slow again from Santner.

That’s a glorious shot from Kohli, uses his bottom hand to hit it straight down the ground for a BOUNDARY! That’s flighted and wide of off stump and Kohli leaves that thinking its a wide but its not given. Kohli reviews it.

Replays show there is some part of the ball inside the wide line, decision stays. RCB lose a review. Oooohh Kohli sweeps that to cow corner and just beyond the outstretched arms of the fielder from deep mid wicket, BOUNDARY!

Markande into the attack, starts with a short one and Salt pulls that in the gap in cow corner for a BOUNDARY! Salt reaches his 50!

Salt cuts that in the deep backward point region for another BOUNDARY! Excellent placement from Salt. Salt cuts that powerfully through the extra cover region for ANOTHER BOUNDARY!

SIX! SALT GOES OVER DEEP SQUARE LEG FOR A MAXIMUM! Three fours and a six off the first four balls! RCB ARE ON FIRE! Googly from Markande, Salt guides it off his pads to deep square leg for a single. Kohli sweeps it deep mid wicket for a single.

Santner bowls his second. Flights that and slows it down nicely, Kohli hits it straight to extra cover, dot ball. Slightly fuller from Santner this time, Kohli hits it to deep cover for a single.

Santner bowls a full toss, Salt hits it to long off for a single. Flighted and wide outside off stump, Kohli fails to connect with the cut shot. Oooohhh Kohli got lucky there, goes for the drive but inside edges it to short fine, that missed the stumps only by a whisker. Salt takes a single on the off side. Good comeback from Santner after an expensive first over.

Bumrah with his second. Starts with a full toss, Kohli can’t capitalise and can hit it only straight to mid on. Santner with the direct hit but Salt is back in his crease. Kohli flicks it on the leg side for a single.

Salt guides it straight to short third, dot ball. Salt flicks it nicely off his pads on the leg side but good work in the deep from Tilak Varma to keep it down to two. Slower bouncer from Bumrah and that’s too short, wide given.

Another slower one from Bumrah, catches Salt slightly off guard but he manages to hit it to mid on for a quick single. Full from Bumrah, Kohli flicks it to deep fine leg for a single.

Mitchell Santner into the attack. Real loosener to start, and gets the treatment , Salt pulls it for a MAXIMUM! SALT GOES AGAIN! THIS TIME OVER MID ON FOR ANOTHER SIX!

Another loose short ball from Santner and Salt pulls it for ANOTHER SIX! THREE SIXES ON THE TROT! Santner flights that nicely but it gets the same treatment, Salt sweeps it past mid on for a BOUNDARY! Already 22 runs off the over. Santner goes full and angles that towards his pads, Salt hits it straight to short fine leg, dot ball.

Santner slows it and flights that nicely, Salt hits it straight to cover, two dot balls to finish. Expensive first over from Santner.

Bumrah into the attack, starts with a full toss, Kohli tries to flick it but it hits his pads.

Ooohhh slower bouncer from Bumrah and Kohli upper cuts it just above short third for a BOUNDARY!

Bumrah with a quick one hits Kohli’s pads and MI appeals but it looks like its sliding down leg side, one leg bye. Replays show there was an inside egde as well. Salt pulls it on the leg side for two runs.

Oooohhh good yorker from Bumrah, right on the money, Salt does well to get bat on that to mid on for a single. Kohli tries to guide that backward point but is stopped, its only a single.

Boult with his second, glorious shot from Kohli! Steps out of his crease and goes down the ground for a BOUNDARY! Kohli hits it to backward point for a single.

Ooohhh Salt tries to pull that and it hits his body, dot ball. Salt is in slight discomfort, the physios are out to check if he is okay. Play resumes. Salt pulls that powerfully for a SIX! HE GETS THE PULL SHOT RIGHT THIS TIME!

Low full toss from Boult, Salt hits that straight to mid wicket, dot ball. Salt reaches 350 T20 sixes with that maximum. Bouncer from Boult, Salt fails to hit that.

Hardik Pandya to bowl the second over. Pandya starts with a short ball, Salt does not connect with the pull and the ball hits the bottom of the bat, dot ball.

Ooohhh, Salt does not time that and flicks it just above mid on for two runs. Shot from Salt, goes through covers for a BOUNDARY! Powerfully struck!

Full and wide down the leg side from Pandya, he’ll have to bowl that one again. Salt hits it to covers and takes a quick single. Kohli on strike. Kohli drives that straight to extra cover, dot ball.

Dead ball, Kohli walks away as he is distracted by the side screen. Kohli defends and takes a quick single.

Virat Kohli and Phil Salt walk out to the middle for RCB. Trent Boult takes the new ball for MI. The players and the stadium observe a minute of silence for Asha Bhosle.

Salt on strike. Slip in place. Ooohhh action already on the first ball, Salt takes a quick single by hitting it to mid on but Tilak Varma with the direct hit. Salt is comfortably safe though.

Inswinger from Boult and that hits Kohli’s pads, but its too high for the LBW. What a shot from Kohli! Flicks it on the leg side all the way for a SIX! Kohli charges and tries to go big again, but it hits the inside part of his bat and goes to deep fine leg for a single.

Salt creates room and smashes it through the covers for a BOUNDARY! Salt cuts it to point for a single.

RCB will be looking to post a 200 plus total on a good batting surface against MI. But can MI’s bowlers restrict RCB’s batters and can Jasprit Bumrah rediscover his form? This promises to be a swashbuckling contest. IPL fans, are you ready? Here we go...

After all that noise about Josh Hazlewood’s injury and his availability, the Australian finally made his comeback in the last match against Rajasthan Royals, much to the delight of the RCB camp. However, after just one game, Hazlewood was dropped for his replacement Jacob Duffy against MI today, who impressed in his two outings when Hazlewood was not fit to play. The reasons for his omission are unclear.

Jacob Bethell, Rasikh Dar, Swapnil Singh, Mangesh Yadav, Venkatesh Iyer

Sherfane Rutherford, Corbin Bosch, Raj Bawa, Raghu Sharma, Ashwani Kumar

Virat Kohli, Phil Salt, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar, Jitesh Sharma, Tim David, Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Suyash Sharma, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Jacob Duffy

Rohit Sharma, Ryan Rickelton, Suryakumar Yadav, Tilak Varma, Hardik Pandya, Naman Dhir, Mitchell Santner, Shardul Thakur, Mayank Markande, Trent Boult, Jasprit Bumrah

MI captain Hardik Pandya says it looks like a good pitch, always a good idea to bat second Wankhede. MI makes two changes, Santner in for Ghazanfar, Markande in for Chahar.

RCB captain Rajat Patidar said RCB will try and put scoreboard pressure on MI by batting first. RCB make one change, Duffy in for Hazlewood, interesting.

It’s a pitch that’s going to produce a lot of runs according to the experts. The pitch is hard and has a even covering of grass meaning there’s going to be some assistance for the fast bowlers as well. With the dew expected to play a role in the second innings, the team which wins the toss is expected to bowl first.

Virat Kohli, Phil Salt, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar, Jitesh Sharma, Tim David, Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Suyash Sharma, Abhinandan Singh, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Josh Hazlewood

Rohit Sharma, Ryan Rickelton, Suryakumar Yadav, Tilak Varma, Hardik Pandya, Sherfane Rutherford, Naman Dhir, Mitchell Santner, Deepak Chahar, Shardul Thakur, Jasprit Bumrah, Trent Boult.

Rajat Patidar (c), Tim David, Virat Kohli, Devdutt Padikkal, Philip Salt, Jitesh Sharma, Jacob Bethell, Krunal Pandya, Romario Shepherd, Abhinandan Singh, Josh Hazlewood, Rasikh Salam Dar, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Suyash Sharma, Swapnil Singh, Nuwan Thushara, Venkatesh Iyer, Jacob Duffy, Jordan Cox, Vicky Ostwal, Vihaan Malhotra, Kanishk Chouhan, Mangesh Yadav, Satvik Deswal

Hardik Pandya (C), Rohit Sharma, Suryakumar Yadav, Jasprit Bumrah, Tilak Varma, Quinton de Kock, Ryan Rickelton, Trent Boult, Deepak Chahar, Shardul Thakur, Will Jacks, Sherfane Rutherford, Mitchell Santner, Allah Ghazanfar, Corbin Bosch, Mayank Markande, Naman Dhir, Robin Minz, Raj Angad Bawa, Atharva Ankolekar, Mayank Rawat, Ashwani Kumar, Raghu Sharma, and Mohammad Izhar.

At the Wankhede, dew is “almost always” expected to play a role during the second innings, which heavily favors the team batting second.

Despite Wankhede being the home ground for Mumbai Indians, Kohli’s massive popularity often turns the stadium into a “theatre” for his performances, with the crowd frequently cheering for him over the home team.

Last season, the noise levels at the venue reportedly peaked at 138 decibels when he walked out to bat.

The Wankhede Stadium has consistently been a “happy hunting ground” for the former RCB captain. His career stats at the venue are:

In the pre-match presser Pollard emphasized that while Rohit and Virat are stalwarts of the game, the match is a collective effort between MI and RCB rather than a “Rohit-Kohli show.

In 33 innings, Rohit Sharma has scored a total of 848 runs against RCB at an average of 27.35 and a strike rate of 136.99. His highest individual score against them is 94, which he achieved in 2018. Overall, he has recorded seven half-centuries against the franchise.

Kohli enters this match in good rhythm, having already scored 129 runs in three innings this season.

Overall, he has scored 922 runs against Mumbai Indians in his IPL career and sits on a total of 8,790 IPL runs.

Most Runs: Virat Kohli leads the charts with 980 runs in 35 innings, followed by AB de Villiers (693) and Rohit Sharma (629).

Most Wickets: Jasprit Bumrah holds the record for the most wickets taken in matches between these two franchises.

Highest Strike Rate: Among top run-getters, Suryakumar Yadav boasts the highest strike rate at 163.38.

With Rohit turning 39 and Kohli 38, Pollard dismissed concerns about their age. He cited examples like MS Dhoni (44) and Imran Tahir (46+) to argue that players should be honored for their contributions and allowed to decide their own retirement timelines.

Tonight’s clash will see the two biggest names in Indian cricket face off against each other- Virat Kohli and Jasprit Bumrah. Kohli has scored 150 runs from 17 innings against Bumrah and the pace spearhead has dismissed Kohli five times.

One might argue that Kohli goes into tonight’s clash with the upperhand considering Bumrah has gone wicket less from his first three matches but the MI pacer can never be discounted.

The focus will be on the return of Mitchell Santner today. After missing Mumbai Indians’ (MI) last game against Rajasthan Royals due to a “minor niggle,” the New Zealand skipper has regained full fitness and was seen bowling long spells in training

RCB and MI have two of the strongest squads this season and tonight promises to be a mouthwatering clash at a pivotal point of the season. The rivalry has historically witnessed many intense and high voltage moments and the presence of many high profile names across both teams will only add to the excitement.

RCB edged out MI by 12 runs in a high scoring contest the last time the two sides faced off against each other. Incidentally, that match also took place at the Wankhede Stadium, the venue of tonight’s clash.

Sixties from Virat Kohli and Rajat Patidar helped RCB post 221 before MI came within 12 runs off the target thanks to Tilak Varma’s quickfire 56 but eventually fell short of the target. Will we witness another high scoring contest tonight?

Royal Challengers Bengaluru aims to exploit MI’s lack of bowling control, though it face its own selection dilemma regarding whether to start Abhinandan Singh or Rasikh Dar.

Mumbai Indians returns to Wankhede Stadium struggling with its “slow starters” tag after losing two consecutive away games and seeing its bowling unit struggle for wickets.

Welcome to Sportstar’s LIVE Blog of the MI vs RCB IPL 2026 match.

title: Rockstar Games breach: Hackers warn of massive data leak
content:
Rockstar Games has confirmed a data breach involving some of its information. The company said the compromise was due to a breach of a third-party provider.
The hacking group ShinyHunters has claimed responsibility for the incident, saying they accessed Rockstar's Snowflake instances through Anodot, a cost-monitoring and analytics service.
They are demanding a ransom by April 14 or they will leak the stolen data.

In a statement to Kotaku, Rockstar Games assured that the compromised data was limited in scope.
The company stressed that "this incident has no impact on our organization or our players."
However, it is still unclear what specific information was compromised in the hack.
It seems likely that corporate data rather than player information was targeted by the attackers.

While the exact nature of the compromised data remains unknown, there are speculations that it could include financial records, marketing data, or contracts with companies like Sony and Microsoft.
This is not the first time Rockstar Games has been targeted by a high-profile hack.
In 2022, a massive cache of videos from GTA VI was leaked online by Lapsus$.

title: Exercise Intensity Over Volume Linked to Lower Disease Risks
content:
People who engaged in more vigorous physical activity during the week had lower risks for many chronic diseases and death, regardless of the total volume of activity. Intensity mattered more than volume of activity for most conditions, although metabolic conditions benefited from both.

“[The study] suggests that comprehensive activity programs incorporating both sufficient volume and intensity remain optimal for metabolic health, unlike the intensity-focused strategies that may be most effective for immune-mediated and other strongly intensity-dependent conditions,” the researchers of the study wrote.

The study was led by Jiehua Wei, Xiangya School of Public Health, Central South University, Changsha, China. It was published online on March 29 in the European Heart Journal.

Residual confounding and errors in self-reporting could not be ruled out. Activity was recorded for a single 7-day period, which may not have reflected long-term behavior. The cohort was mainly White with a low response rate.

Several authors reported receiving support from multiple research organizations and foundations, including the National Natural Science Foundation of China and the Fundamental and Interdisciplinary Disciplines Breakthrough Plan of the Ministry of Education of China. One author reported serving as a paid consultant and holding equity in Compliment 1, a US-based commercial entity developing products related to physical activity and chronic disease prevention.

This article was created using several editorial tools, including AI, as part of the process. Human editors reviewed this content before publication.

title: iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone Fold Specs Tipped Ahead Of Launch - Gizbot News
content:
A new tip from Debayan Roy (@Gadgetsdata) sheds more light on Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro, while also bringing Apple’s foldable iPhone back into the conversation.

According to the tip, the iPhone 18 Pro may feature a 48MP main camera with variable aperture, which means the lens can adjust how much light enters depending on the scene. This could help in low light by letting in more light, while also reducing overexposure in bright conditions.

There’s also mention of a 48MP periscope telephoto camera, possibly paired with a larger aperture. If that holds, it could improve low-light zoom performance and overall image clarity at longer focal lengths.

On the front, the camera could move to 24MP, suggesting Apple may be planning a noticeable bump for selfies and video calls.

The leak also points to a 2nm A20 Pro chip, along with a new C2 modem. While performance numbers aren’t mentioned, the shift to 2nm typically signals a focus on both efficiency and power gains.

Battery is another area that stands out. The iPhone 18 Pro is tipped to feature a battery capacity above 5000mAh, which would mark a step up compared to current models.

The device could also support 5G via satellite, expanding connectivity in areas where traditional networks may struggle.

In terms of design, the leak suggests Apple isn’t making major changes this time. The iPhone 18 Pro is expected to retain the Dynamic Island and a similar overall camera layout, with new color options likely being the more visible update.

It also notes that LTPO+ may not be part of the package, despite earlier speculation.

Alongside the Pro model, the tip briefly outlines Apple’s foldable iPhone.

It’s described as a book-style foldable with a 5.5-inch outer display and a 7.8-inch inner screen. The device could feature a liquid metal hinge and a near crease-free display, along with a slim 4.5mm profile when unfolded.

The foldable model is also expected to use the same A20 Pro chip and C2 modem, with Touch ID instead of Face ID.

The leak points to a launch around September, which aligns with Apple’s usual iPhone release cycle.

Explore the latest in technology, gadgets, and innovation. Stay updated with smartphone launches, reviews, comparisons, and expert insights.

title: Want garage for your toy cars? Parksible is the answer
content:
Fun-Tech-Lab, the company behind the popular Windsible mini wind tunnel, has launched a new product: the Parksible.
The high-tech display case is specifically designed for 1:64-scale model cars like Hot Wheels.
It features an automated parking system with space for up to 14 cars and even comes with an app to manage your car collection on-the-go.

The Parksible is a modern take on the classic toy car parking garages that many automotive enthusiasts grew up with.
It may not be directly useful, but its appeal lies in its ability to bring back childhood memories of organizing toy cars.
The company has already seen huge success with this product on Kickstarter, raising nearly half a million dollars against an initial goal of just over $11,000.

The Parksible isn't just a pretty face; it also controls temperature and humidity to keep your cars in top shape.
The premium version of the display even comes with an app that scans your cars as you load them, creating a 3D model of each one.
This way, you can carry your own virtual parking garage wherever you go, adding another layer of fun to this innovative product.

The Parksible is priced at $499, making it a premium choice for collectors with large toy car collections.
The product has been well received by the market, with many backers already supporting its Kickstarter campaign.
If you're looking for a unique desktop ornament and have a soft spot for model cars, now is the time to check out this innovative product from Fun-Tech-Lab.

title: Samsung likely to expand foldable line-up with new ‘Wide’ Galaxy Z Fold model – Firstpost
content:
The foldable smartphone story has often been one of promise tempered by compromise. Larger screens and futuristic designs have drawn attention, but everyday usability has remained a sticking point. Now, Samsung’s rumoured Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide suggests the company may be ready to change that narrative.

Rather than chasing experimental form factors, Samsung appears to be leaning into refinement. Early reports indicate that the “Wide” variant could prioritise practicality, with design tweaks aimed at making foldables less of a novelty and more of a dependable daily device. If accurate, this could signal a broader shift in how the category evolves.

At the centre of the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is a rumoured 4:3 aspect ratio for its inner display, a notable departure from the narrower layouts seen in earlier foldables. This wider, more balanced screen is expected to bring the experience closer to that of a tablet, echoing devices like the Apple iPad.

The implications are significant. A 4:3 display could make tasks such as reading, typing and multitasking feel more natural, reducing the awkward scaling issues that have long affected foldables. For users juggling emails, documents and media, the shift may translate into a more intuitive workflow.

Ergonomics are also likely to benefit. A shorter, wider design could make the device easier to handle when unfolded, addressing complaints about tall, unwieldy screens. By narrowing the gap between a smartphone and a tablet, Samsung may be positioning the Fold 8 Wide as a true hybrid device suited to both productivity and entertainment.

Another key area of improvement appears to be the outer display. Foldables have often struggled here, with cover screens that feel secondary or limited. The Fold 8 Wide, however, is expected to feature an upgraded outer screen that mirrors the capabilities of the inner display more closely.

If realised, this could reduce the need to constantly unfold the device for simple tasks like replying to messages or checking notifications. In practical terms, that means a smoother, more efficient everyday experience, one where users can rely on the cover screen without compromise.

Seamless transitions between the inner and outer displays are also said to be a focus. Samsung is reportedly working to ensure that switching between screens feels fluid, without interruptions or inconsistencies. This could enhance multitasking, allowing users to move between apps and contexts with minimal friction.

Taken together, these changes point to a device that is less about spectacle and more about polish. It reflects a maturing category, where incremental improvements in usability may matter more than dramatic design experiments.

The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is expected to debut at Samsung’s next Galaxy Unpacked event, reportedly scheduled for 22 July 2026 in London. The event is also likely to see the launch of the standard Galaxy Z Fold 8, alongside potential updates to the company’s wearable lineup, including new Galaxy Watch models.

While there is no official confirmation yet, the timing aligns with Samsung’s typical release cycle for foldables. The company has consistently used its mid-year Unpacked events to showcase its latest innovations in this category.

The launch comes at a crucial moment. Competition in the foldable market is intensifying, particularly in China, where manufacturers are pushing aggressive designs and pricing strategies. At the same time, speculation continues to build around a foldable iPhone from Apple, expected later in 2026.

In this context, Samsung’s approach with the Fold 8 Wide could be strategic. By focusing on usability and refinement, the company may be aiming to set a new benchmark before rivals enter the space in force.

There are also hints of additional features, including the possible return of S Pen support, though details remain unclear. If included, this could further strengthen the device’s appeal for productivity-focused users.

Ultimately, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide may represent a subtle but important evolution. Instead of redefining what a foldable can look like, it seeks to redefine how it fits into everyday life. And that, more than any headline-grabbing feature, could determine whether foldables finally go mainstream.

OpenAI chief Sam Altman's San Francisco home faced two attacks in two days: a Molotov cocktail incident followed by a suspected firearm discharge. Police arrested suspects in both cases, highlighting rising tensions around AI's societal impact.
